notes: Chiefs entered offseason ~$57M over cap. Mahomes restructure (~$43.56M savings), Taylor release ($20M savings), Danna cut ($9M savings), and McDuffie trade ($13.6M savings) collectively cleared cap room before and during FA. Cap space per OverTheCap as of March 13.

key_departures.impact: Two-time All-Pro, the Chiefs' best CB and second-best overall defender behind Chris Jones. Elite nickel and boundary corner, 2024 second-team All-Pro. Massive void at CB1/slot.
Starting CB2, 15-game starter in 2025 with 64 tackles, 2 INTs, 6 PDs. Lost to division rival Rams alongside McDuffie. Chiefs now without both starting CBs from their 2022 draft class.

Starting safety, career-high 83.5 PFF grade in 2025. Physical, versatile box/deep hybrid. Combined with McDuffie/Watson departures, Chiefs lost three defensive starters in one week. Critical loss at S.

Versatile 2-time SB champion LB who started 44 games. 58 tackles, 2 sacks, 5 QB hits in 2025. Never exceeded 53% of defensive snaps in KC but was an impactful run defender and blitzer. Chiefs retain Nick Bolton and Drue Tranquill (restructured), so LB is less of a crisis.
Rotational edge rusher, down year in 2025. Departure reduces already thin EDGE depth behind George Karlaftis and Chris Jones. Escalates a pre-existing EDGE need.
Former RB1 but injury-hampered in 2025 (3.9 YPC, 462 yards in 13 games). Replacement (Kenneth Walker III) is a clear upgrade. Departure has no negative need impact — need was already being addressed.
Third CB from the 2022 draft class to depart. Depth/special teams contributor in 2025. Part of the broader secondary exodus — Chiefs lost four DBs in one week.
Backup QB lost with Mahomes recovering from torn ACL. Chiefs need a reliable backup. Creates/escalates a secondary QB need.
Starting RT released for cap savings ($20M cleared). Persistent penalty problems and declining play made the cut easier. Creates a hole at OT.
Cut for $9M in cap savings after down year in 2025. Compounds the thin EDGE situation behind Karlaftis.
Veteran rotational DT, minimal impact. Departure somewhat addressed by Tonga signing.

signings.analysis: Reigning Super Bowl LX MVP ($28.7M fully guaranteed on $43.05M base). The richest free-agent RB deal at guaranteed money in NFL history at signing. At $14.35M AAV, elite tier for RB. Directly replaces the injury-prone Pacheco with a clear upgrade — 61 forced missed tackles in 2025, 10 rushes of 20+ yards. Three-year commitment takes him through age-28 season. RB need is fully resolved. Depth piece (Demercado) adds further insurance.
signings.draft_note: Signing does not preclude a late-round RB pick but eliminates any urgency.

signings.analysis: Signed same night Cook departed to Bengals. Three-year deal ($15M fully guaranteed) signals a real investment at the position. At $8.25M AAV, mid-tier starter money — reasonable given Gilman is 29 and coming off a midseason trade context. PFF ranked him 24th among qualifying safeties in 2025 (1,026 snaps, 68 tackles, 6 PBUs). Versatile box/deep player. S need partially mitigated — Gilman is a capable starter but a lateral/slight downgrade from Cook's peak. Chiefs will likely still target S in the draft for long-term depth, but acute need is resolved.
signings.draft_note: A 3-year deal buys stability. Still prudent to add S depth in mid rounds.

signings.analysis: Three-year deal at $7M AAV — mid-tier for IDL. Career year with Patriots in 2025: 24 tackles, 2 TFLs, 2 QB hits in 14 games. Rare run-stuffing nose tackle type the Chiefs have lacked since Dontari Poe per team sources. Partners Chris Jones (pass rush/interior). Addresses IDL depth meaningfully — prior to this signing, Omarr Norman-Lott (2025 ACL) was the only notable option. Chris Jones remains the anchor. Tonga pairing with Jones solidifies the interior but KC still has limited IDL depth beyond these two and likely needs a developmental body in the draft.
signings.draft_note: Partially addresses IDL depth. A mid-round IDL pick remains useful.

signings.analysis: Re-signed for a 14th season on a prove-it 1-year deal at $12M (up to $15M). Kelce led the team in 2025 with 108 targets, 76 receptions, 851 yards, 5 TDs. The fact that it's a 1-year deal at age 37 is the key signal — this is a bridge, not a long-term solution. TE remains a mid-tier need for the future. Kelce keeps the position functional for 2026, but Chiefs MUST address TE long-term, likely in the 2026 or 2027 draft.
signings.draft_note: 1-year deal at 37 means TE is still a legitimate mid-round draft target for the future.

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 2-year/$11M deal (max $14M). Thornton, 25, was the team's best WR in stretches of 2025 — 438 receiving yards, 3 TDs in 14 games while Worthy and Rice were out. Deep-threat specialist, but at $5.5M AAV this is depth/WR2 money, not WR1 money. Chiefs still have Xavier Worthy (returning from shoulder surgery) and Rashee Rice (uncertain immediate future). WR remains a real need — especially for a team with a recovering QB who needs proven weapons. Thornton re-signing only partially addresses the position.
signings.draft_note: Guarantees on not yet reported. WR remains a tier-2 draft need, particularly if Rice's status remains uncertain.

signings.analysis: One-year prove-it deal (terms not yet fully disclosed; Spotrac MV ~$2M/yr). Coming off torn ACL that cost him all of 2025. Was a 38-game starter for Miami before the injury. Projection is nickel corner — helps fill the slot void left by McDuffie's departure. However, this is a discount/injury-rebound gamble. CB remains an acute, tier-1 need. The Chiefs lost McDuffie (All-Pro), Watson (starter), and Williams (depth) in this cycle. Kohou + Kristian Fulton + Nohl Williams is NOT an adequate CB room for a contender. The draft must address CB early.
signings.draft_note: Kohou signing does NOT resolve the CB need. This is a bridge move. CB remains the top priority in the 2026 draft.

signings.analysis: One-year depth signing behind Kenneth Walker III. Former Cardinals UDFA (TCU, 2023) who went untendered by Arizona. Provides RB competition behind Walker and ahead of Brashard Smith/ShunDerrick Powell. Contract terms not yet disclosed. Low-cost depth move — does not change the RB need picture (Walker signing already fully addressed it), but adds roster insurance.
Re-signed as a special teams contributor and LB depth piece. Minimal impact on positional need assessment. Does not address the LB starter void left by Chenal's departure.
12th season in KC. Re-signing of critical special teams anchor preserves the Butker/Winchester/Araiza chemistry. No positional need impact.
ERFA tender at $1.075M non-guaranteed. Standard retention move. No positional need impact.
ERFA tender at $1.075M non-guaranteed. Fringe roster/special teams piece. Does not address the WR need picture.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Lost ALL THREE starting-caliber CBs from the 2022 class: McDuffie (trade, All-Pro), Watson (UFA, starter), Williams (UFA, depth). Added only Kohou (1-year, post-ACL prove-it) to a group of Kristian Fulton, Nohl Williams, Chamarri Conner, Kevin Knowles II. CB is now the single most critical draft need — tier 1. Must target CB in the first round (hold picks No. 9 and 29). The team explicitly has no proven starter-quality CB outside of Fulton and the unproven Nohl Williams.

Kenneth Walker III signing (3yr/$43.05M, $28.7M guaranteed) is the largest guaranteed RB free-agent contract in NFL history. Walker is a top-5 RB — 61 forced missed tackles, Super Bowl MVP. RB need is fully resolved through 2028. Depth added via Demercado. No draft capital needed here.

Bryan Cook loss (3yr/$40.25M to Bengals) was significant — a young, top-tier safety. Alohi Gilman replacement (3yr/$24.75M, $15M gtd) is a competent starter but a modest downgrade. Gilman is 29 and this is a 3-year bridge at the position. Safety remains a tier-3 need for the future. Chiefs should add S in rounds 3-5 for long-term depth.

Jawaan Taylor released (cap-driven cut). No OT signed in free agency. Taylor was the starting RT for three seasons despite penalty issues. Chiefs now have a clear vacancy at RT with no replacement identified. OT escalates to tier 2 — should address via the draft or remaining free agency.

Lost both Omenihu (UFA, Commanders) and Danna (cut) from the rotational group. George Karlaftis is the clear starter alongside Chris Jones. The market for elite edge rushers was picked over (Mafe to Bengals, Oweh re-signed by Chargers). No EDGE signed in FA. Chiefs need EDGE depth — still a tier-2 need, possibly via draft rounds 2-3.

Khyiris Tonga signing (3yr/$21M, $14M gtd) pairs with Chris Jones as a legitimate starter duo. Tonga is a run-stuffing nose tackle who fills a long-standing void. IDL drops from tier 2 to tier 3. Depth remains thin (Norman-Lott returning from ACL is an unknown), so a mid-round IDL pick remains smart but not urgent.

Thornton re-sign (2yr/$11M) retains a proven deep threat at 25 years old. Worthy returns from shoulder surgery. Rice's status remains uncertain. No WR addition was made. The room is still thin — only 2 proven contributors — and a WR2/slot target would significantly help Mahomes in his return from ACL. WR stays at tier 2.

Travis Kelce re-signed on a 1-year/$12M deal. He remains an elite target for Mahomes at age 37, leading the team in every receiving category in 2025. However, the 1-year structure at 37 means this is a bridge. TE is a future need — tier 3 for 2026 draft (rounds 3-5 for developmental piece), escalating to tier 1 or 2 in 2027.

Leo Chenal departed (3yr/$24.75M, Commanders). Nick Bolton and Drue Tranquill (restructured deal, saved $2.5M) remain. Bolton and Tranquill are quality starters but Chenal's versatility (edge rush, run stuffing, spy role) is difficult to replace cheaply. LB depth is thin. Escalates to tier 2 — should draft a LB in rounds 3-4.

Gardner Minshew departed (1yr/$8.25M, Cardinals). Patrick Mahomes is recovering from a torn ACL (December 2025) with an uncertain return timeline — could miss early 2026 season games. The Chiefs currently have no viable backup QB on the roster. This is an urgent, short-term need. They must add a veteran QB (free agent or trade) and/or draft a QB in the mid-rounds.

key_additions.headline: Super Bowl LX MVP signs richest guaranteed RB deal in FA history, instantly solving KC's biggest offensive problem
Versatile 3-year starter replaces Bryan Cook and anchors a rebuilt Chiefs secondary
Run-stuffing nose tackle gives Chris Jones a true partner inside, the best IDL depth KC has had in years
All-time great returns for 14th season with team that drafted him, led Chiefs in every receiving category in 2025
Deep-threat re-sign keeps an ascending 25-year-old who posted career-high 438 yards when Worthy/Rice were hurt
38-game Dolphins starter adds veteran competition at nickel CB, returning from torn ACL that cost him all of 2025
Low-cost depth piece adds insurance behind Kenneth Walker III in an improved KC backfield

key_losses.headline: Two-time All-Pro CB traded for a draft haul — Chiefs land picks No. 9 and 29, recouping roster capital over a $120M+ extension
Starting CB2 joins McDuffie in LA, completing the departure of both starters from KC's 2022 draft secondary class
Career-high 83.5 PFF grade safety returns to hometown, leaving the biggest individual void of KC's secondary purge
Versatile 2-time SB champion LB departs, leaving KC without their most dynamic run-defending linebacker
Starting RT cut for cap relief, opening a hole on KC's right side that remains unaddressed in free agency
Backup QB departs as Mahomes recovers from torn ACL, leaving KC without a capable backup in a precarious situation
Rotational edge rusher departs, compounding thin EDGE depth behind only George Karlaftis

contract_confidence_notes.Kader Kohou: Confirmed — Jordan Schultz/ESPN agents confirmed; exact dollar terms not yet publicly disclosed
contract_confidence_notes.Emari Demercado: Reported — Jordan Schultz; terms not yet disclosed
